claim: Filed against the prior (8888/key=value) iteration's traceability matrix, which pointed at src/main/services.ts and src/main/ipc/register-orky.ts (untouched) instead of the real composition root src/main/ipc/register.ts, and missed src/shared/types.ts as OrkyHeartbeat's home.

claim: Filed against the prior (8888/key=value) iteration's body decode: the 256-char cap was measured with body.length (UTF-16 units) after splitting into pairs, so an unknown forward-compat pair could carry non-ASCII/control bytes past a byte-accurate cap.
fix: Carried forward as binding REQ-007 in the rewritten 02-spec.md: cap must be measured as actual UTF-8 byte length, before JSON.parse.
verified_by: security (2026-06-30): REQ-007 is correctly implemented in the new code at src/main/status/orky-osc-parser.ts:75-79 — `Buffer.byteLength(payload, 'utf8')` checked strictly before `JSON.parse`. TEST-016 (emoji-padded reason, .length under cap but UTF-8 bytes over) confirms rejection. The old key=value grammar this finding was filed against no longer exists. RE-VERIFIED security (loopback iter3, 2026-06-30): this iteration's diff is scoped entirely to src/shared/orky-status.ts's heartbeatKind() (REQ-009 kind-derivation, FINDING-DA-004/ESC-002) plus test/fixture/spec files; orky-osc-parser.ts (decodeHeartbeat, lines 75-79) is byte-for-byte unchanged in `git show HEAD`. Re-ran tests/main/orky-osc-parser.test.ts (20/20 green, incl. TEST-016) against the current worktree — still correctly resolved, no regression introduced by this iteration.

location: src/main/status/orky-osc-parser.ts (OLD, now-replaced parser) — root cause in src/main/status/osc-scanner.ts (scanOsc, shared/pre-existing)
claim: Filed against the prior iteration: scanOsc's no-terminator branch returns the entire accumulated buffer, not a bounded carry-over, so a 12-byte prefix followed by sustained non-terminating PTY output caused unbounded heap growth in the privileged Electron main process.
fix: Carried forward as binding REQ-006 in the rewritten 02-spec.md: explicit MAX_PENDING_BYTES ceiling enforced independently of scanOsc's carry-over.
verified_by: security (2026-06-30): REQ-006 is correctly implemented in the new code at src/main/status/orky-osc-parser.ts:111-126 — `MAX_PENDING_BYTES = 8192` enforced after every push, independent of scanOsc, margin verified correct (7 + 4096 + 2 = 4105 < 8192, so no in-flight legal marker can be dropped). TEST-014 pushes 6 MiB across 96 chunks and asserts the internal buffer stays bounded (not just no-throw). devils-advocate review (2026-06-30) independently confirmed this arithmetic. RE-VERIFIED security (loopback iter3, 2026-06-30): this iteration's diff (`git show HEAD`) touches only src/shared/orky-status.ts's heartbeatKind() — orky-osc-parser.ts's push()/MAX_PENDING_BYTES guard (lines 111-126) is byte-for-byte unchanged. Re-ran tests/main/orky-osc-parser.test.ts (20/20 green, incl. TEST-014 asserting the buffer stays ≤ MAX_PENDING_BYTES) against the current worktree — still correctly resolved, no regression introduced by this iteration.

claim: The stateful fs/stream combiner OrkyStreamStatusBridge -- the ONLY event-arrival-order-sensitive component in this feature and the actual production applier of REQ-013's filesystem-wins precedence -- has no behavioral test. Only the pure selectOrkyPaneStatus precedence rule is unit-tested; the bridge that holds per-pane Map state, applies precedence under interleaved fs/stream arrival, dedups via JSON.stringify, and clears on pane teardown is exercised by nothing.
fix: Add a behavioral unit test for OrkyStreamStatusBridge asserting order-independent convergence: drive setFsStatus-then-setStreamHeartbeat and setStreamHeartbeat-then-setFsStatus for the same pane id and assert the final emitted status is identical (filesystem wins) regardless of arrival order; assert an unchanged recompute is deduped (no duplicate emit); assert clearPane drops the pane so a later setStreamHeartbeat starts clean.
verified_by: determinism (re-verified 2026-06-30): src/main/orky/orky-stream-status.ts was NOT touched by this implementation pass (consumes the parser's output type, not its wire format) and still has no behavioral test. Code remains order-convergent and correct today, but a future regression (swapped selectOrkyPaneStatus args, dropped lastKey dedup, unioned fs+stream) would pass the whole suite undetected.

claim: Filed against the prior iteration's key=value field-collection loop: a repeated key silently kept only the last occurrence, undocumented and untested.
fix: N/A — the body grammar is now JSON, not key=value pairs.
verified_by: quality (2026-06-30): the new decodeHeartbeat calls JSON.parse once and reads only seven named fields off the result; duplicate-key resolution is now governed by ECMA-262's well-defined JSON.parse semantics (last-occurrence-wins is standard language behavior), not a hand-rolled merge loop. No analogous issue exists in the new code.

claim: A well-formed heartbeat marker remains fundamentally UNAUTHENTICATED. Any process that can write to the PTY can fabricate a status for an arbitrary feature slug — including a fake needsHuman:true to draw operator attention to a non-event, or a fake low-urgency state to mask a real one. REQ-004 explicitly mandates verbatim trust (Termhalla is a thin client, never 'corrects' Orky's verdict) — by design, not a bug, but the trust boundary is not documented anywhere. Additionally, `reason` is free-form attacker prose rendered verbatim into Termhalla's chrome via heartbeatDetail, and a spoofed needsHuman:true rides the same orky:status channel that drives border/tab-badge/notification alerts in 0004's presentation — not 'purely cosmetic' in Termhalla even though ADR-026 frames it that way on the Orky side.
fix: State the trust boundary explicitly in docs/features/orky-osc-heartbeat.md (stream-derived status is only as trustworthy as whatever writes to the PTY) and visually distinguish stream-derived (untrusted) status from filesystem-derived (authoritative) status so a spoofed bare-SSH heartbeat is not presented identically to a verified local one.
verified_by: devils-advocate (re-verified 2026-06-30 against the new ADR-026/JSON code — concern persists, relocated to current file:line)

claim: REQ-013's 'filesystem wins whenever present' guarantee holds only spatially (a null check in selectOrkyPaneStatus), not temporally. OrkyTracker.watch() is async (awaits findOrkyRoot then reread before its first fs emit) and unwatch() emits fs=null for a still-live pane. For a LOCAL pane that DOES have a .orky/ tree, a heartbeat decoded from the stream during the fs-null window (startup before first reread, or after a mid-life unwatch) makes the bridge emit a lower-trust stream-derived status on a pane 0004 is supposed to own exclusively. Latent today only because no real Orky-side emitter existed yet at the time of the original finding; now that ADR-026 emission is real and opt-in, it activates the moment a local Orky pane with config.heartbeat.osc enabled streams a marker during one of these windows.
fix: Make the bridge suppress stream-derived status for any pane known to be (or to have ever been) filesystem-watched, rather than relying on fs being momentarily non-null: track per-pane whether an orky:watch is active and prefer last-known fs ownership through transient fs-null emits.
verified_by: devils-advocate (re-verified 2026-06-30 — orky-stream-status.ts/orky-tracker.ts unchanged by this pass, concern persists)

claim: Filed against the prior iteration: rejecting the whole marker on an unrecognized version was in tension with the forward-compat unknown-key-ignore policy, risking a silent total blackout on an additive Orky-side version bump.
fix: REQ-005 in the rewritten spec explicitly reverses this: forward-compatible, not fail-closed, on version + unknown fields.
verified_by: devils-advocate (2026-06-30): decodeHeartbeat does no `v` gating at all (src/main/status/orky-osc-parser.ts:75-101) — v:2, missing v, and unknown fields all decode (TEST-011/012/013). The blackout risk is genuinely fixed (residual risk of silent misrender on a breaking v bump tracked separately as FINDING-DA-006, LOW, non-blocking).

location: src/shared/orky-status.ts:352-356 (heartbeatKind) vs. C:/dev/Orky/plugin/gatekeeper/gatekeeper.js:887-910 (oscHeartbeat, the real emitter) and CLAUDE.md's 'Orky status reads active.json.phase + gate topology — NEVER state.json.phase' gotcha
claim: heartbeatKind derives 'done' only when `hb.phase == null && hb.gateN >= hb.gateM`. Independently verified against the REAL Orky emitter (C:/dev/Orky/plugin/gatekeeper/gatekeeper.js:904): `phase: state.phase || d.phase || null`. Per this repo's own CLAUDE.md gotcha, a fully complete feature's state.json.phase is the string 'doc-sync' (truthy — human-review is tracked as a separate external gate, never written into state.json.phase), so the REAL emitter sends `phase:"doc-sync", gate:"8/8", needsHuman:false` for a genuinely complete feature — never `phase: null`. heartbeatKind's done-branch can therefore NEVER fire for a real completed feature; the chip renders 'busy' forever for any feature that finishes its pipeline while a bare-SSH pane is streaming its heartbeat. This defeats a core scenario the feature exists for (accurate status, including completion, with zero filesystem access). The frozen 02-spec.md's own REQ-009 acceptance criteria only exercise a synthetic `phase: null` complete-heartbeat fixture that likely never occurs on the real wire per the real emitter's logic verified above — i.e. the contradiction is baked into the FROZEN SPEC itself (REQ-009's derivation rule vs. ADR-026's actual `state.phase || d.phase || null` formula), not just an implementation bug; the code correctly implements what the spec says, but what the spec says does not match the real wire.
fix: Re-derive 'done' from gate fullness alone (`gateN >= gateM`) regardless of phase value, mirroring 0004's gate-based isComplete (`f.gates['human-review']?.passed === true`) rather than 0004's phase-null gateFrontier convention (which is a LOCAL Termhalla computation, not what the wire actually carries). Requires a REQ-009 spec correction (loopback to spec) plus a corrected test fixture reflecting the real emitter's `phase:"doc-sync", gate:"8/8"` shape for a complete feature, not the literal-null fixture currently frozen.
verified_by: devils-advocate (loopback iter3, 2026-06-30): GENUINELY RESOLVED, confirmed by an independent re-derivation (not merely 'tests pass'). Re-derived against the real-emitter ground truth — a complete feature ALWAYS has gateN >= gateM regardless of the accompanying phase string, and an incomplete feature NEVER has gateN >= gateM. heartbeatKind (src/shared/orky-status.ts:358-362) now reads `if (hb.needsHuman === true) return 'needs-input'; if (hb.gateN >= hb.gateM) return 'done'; return 'busy'` — the `hb.phase == null &&` clause is gone, so done-detection is purely gate-based and reads `phase` nowhere (grep for `phase == null`/`phase === null` across orky-status.ts: zero matches). The frozen-SPEC contradiction this finding flagged is also gone: REQ-009 was rewritten to mandate gate-based, phase-independent done-detection, and its acceptance now carries the REAL emitter's complete shape `{phase:"doc-sync",gate:"8/8",needsHuman:false}` as the PRIMARY done example (with `phase:null,gate:"8/8"` retained only as a phase-independence companion); TASK-005 (03-plan.md) corrected to match. TEST-040 is a genuine regression guard, not a tautology: it feeds DONE_DOC_SYNC_HEARTBEAT (the real `phase:"doc-sync"` shape) and asserts the clean-done/idle roll-up — under the OLD `phase==null && gateN>=gateM` rule it returned 'busy' (because "doc-sync" !== null short-circuits the AND), so it fails RED on the old code and passes ONLY on the corrected gate-based rule. Ran `npx vitest run tests/shared/orky-heartbeat-status.test.ts`: 12/12 green incl. TEST-040/025/041. The 'busy forever for a completed bare-SSH feature' defect is fixed — a clean-done heartbeat now rolls up to the cleared/idle shape exactly as 0004 does for a completed filesystem feature (clean-done excluded from the popover). Residual: a phase-null label nit on the stream path (a busy/incomplete heartbeat whose phase is null still labels itself 'done' via the retained `?? 'done'` fallback) is tracked separately as FINDING-DA-009 (LOW, non-blocking) — it does NOT reopen DA-004's done-detection defect, which is correctly resolved.

location: src/shared/orky-status.ts:386-401 (heartbeatToFeatureStatus, lastActivityAt hard-coded to 0) + src/main/orky/orky-stream-status.ts (no time input)
claim: The whole premise is a heartbeat (liveness), yet the stream path discards timing entirely: lastActivityAt is hard-coded to 0 and nothing in the parser/bridge uses arrival time. After the remote Orky process exits or the SSH session goes idle — the exact bare-SSH scenario this feature targets — the last needs-human/busy heartbeat stays pinned indefinitely, indistinguishable from a live run. The filesystem path has stall detection (STALL_THRESHOLD_MS/isStalled); the stream path has no equivalent. setStreamHeartbeat(id, null)'s documented clear path is dead code in production (nothing currently calls it with null outside teardown).
fix: Stamp each heartbeat with arrival time and expire/grey stale stream status past a threshold (reuse STALL_THRESHOLD_MS), or clear the stream slot on PTY command-done/idle. Add a REQ + test for stream-status staleness.
verified_by: devils-advocate

claim: REQ-005's forward-compat reversal (FINDING-DA-003) fixed the blackout risk but introduces an unexamined residual: a future v:2 that REDEFINES an existing field's meaning (not just adds one) would be silently decoded with v1 semantics and render WRONG status, not blank status — undetectable cross-repo since the parser never fails closed on version.
fix: Document the residual misrender risk in the REQ-016 cross-repo drift caveat; optionally log-once (not reject) on an unrecognized v for diagnosability.
verified_by: devils-advocate

claim: decodeHeartbeat maps an empty-string `feature` (`{"feature":""}`) to `feature: ""`, not `null`. orkyHeartbeatToPaneStatus's `hb.feature === null` check is then false (not treated as app-loop/cleared), and heartbeatToFeatureStatus's `hb.feature ?? '(unknown)'` does not catch empty string (only null/undefined) — a chip renders with a blank name. Named -OSC to avoid collision with the existing FINDING-DA-007 (0004's null-phase chip guard, a different feature).
fix: Treat empty/whitespace-only feature as null (app-loop) in decodeHeartbeat, or use a truthiness check (`hb.feature || '(unknown)'`) in the mapper.
verified_by: devils-advocate

claim: TASK-007 left orky-stream-status.ts unedited (regression-checked only), so its comments still cite the OLD 13-REQ numbering ('REQ-009' for filesystem-wins, 'REQ-008' for no-new-channel, 'REQ-013' for no-persistence) which under the rewritten 17-REQ spec are actually REQ-013/REQ-012/REQ-017 respectively. selectOrkyPaneStatus's header in orky-status.ts:412 likewise cites the stale '(REQ-009)'.
fix: Update the REQ-id citations in the unchanged bridge/selector comments to the current frozen numbering.
verified_by: devils-advocate

claim: The REQ-006 bounded-ceiling reset makes emission of a LATER valid marker depend on PTY chunk boundaries: for an unterminated-prefix flood exceeding MAX_PENDING_BYTES followed by a later valid marker, whether that later marker parses or is lost (swallowed into one over-long body by scanOsc) depends on exactly when the reset fires relative to chunk delivery. Same on-the-wire bytes can light a status chip on one run/host and not another. Bounded effect (read-only cosmetic status, adversarial input class), hence LOW.
fix: Reconcile the REQ-006 reset with scanOsc's swallow semantics — e.g. retain only a trailing ORKY_OSC-prefix-sized window on reset rather than dropping to empty — and add a behavioral test feeding a flood-then-valid-marker stream at multiple chunk granularities.
verified_by: determinism

claim: `phase: hb.phase as OrkyPhase | null` force-casts OrkyHeartbeat.phase (typed OrkyPhase | string | null specifically because REQ-004 mandates verbatim passthrough of arbitrary strings) into OrkyFeatureStatus.phase's narrower closed union. Currently harmless (the value is only ever stringified, never switched on), but misrepresents the type system's guarantees for any future code that pattern-matches on it.
fix: Widen OrkyFeatureStatus.phase to OrkyPhase | string | null (documenting the heartbeat path's wider range), or introduce a small honest local type instead of the `as` cast.

claim: This iteration (ESC-002/FINDING-DA-004) corrected the misleading 'a null phase implies done' framing everywhere it touched: src/shared/orky-status.ts's file-level wire-shape comment, heartbeatKind()'s JSDoc, and 02-spec.md's reproduced interface excerpt (02-spec.md line 147) all now explicitly distinguish 'null only falls back to the chip LABEL' from 'kind is derived purely from gates, never phase'. But the iteration never touched src/shared/types.ts, which defines the ACTUAL canonical `OrkyHeartbeat` interface that `heartbeatKind(hb: OrkyHeartbeat)` (src/shared/orky-status.ts:358) imports and operates on (`import type { ... OrkyHeartbeat } from './types'`, orky-status.ts:23). Its inline field comment at types.ts:71 still reads verbatim from before this fix: `phase: OrkyPhase | string | null // payload.phase, verbatim; null -> rendered "done"` — the same unqualified 'null -> done' phrasing whose ambiguity (does 'rendered done' mean the LABEL or the `kind`?) is the exact class of misreading this whole iteration exists to correct (the analogous, now-corrected REQ-009 spec wording produced FINDING-DA-004). A future maintainer reading the type's own doc-comment — the single most likely place to consult this field's contract — gets no indication that `kind` is gate-only and may reintroduce a `phase` check into `heartbeatKind`, regressing the exact bug this iteration just fixed.
fix: Update src/shared/types.ts:71's inline comment to the corrected wording already used elsewhere in this iteration's diff, e.g. `phase: OrkyPhase | string | null // payload.phase, verbatim; null only affects the chip LABEL fallback ('done'), never the kind derivation (gate-based only, see heartbeatKind / REQ-009)`.
verified_by: quality (2026-06-30, loopback iter3 review): confirmed via `git show HEAD` that src/shared/types.ts is not in this commit's changed-file list, and via direct read that types.ts:71 still carries the pre-iteration-3 comment while orky-status.ts:341-346/352-357 and 02-spec.md:147 were all updated to the corrected, disambiguated wording. RESOLVED doc-sync (2026-06-30): src/shared/types.ts:71's `OrkyHeartbeat.phase` inline comment rewritten to `payload.phase, verbatim; null only affects the chip LABEL fallback ('done'), never the kind derivation (gate-based only, see heartbeatKind / REQ-009)` — matches the corrected wording used elsewhere in this iteration's diff (orky-status.ts, 02-spec.md). Comment-only change; no logic touched.

claim: The file-header comment at line 6 still describes `kind` as 'DERIVED in `orkyHeartbeatToPaneStatus` from `needsHuman`/`phase`/`gateN`/`gateM` (REQ-009)' — listing `phase` as a `kind`-derivation input. This iteration's ESC-002/FINDING-DA-004 correction made `kind` done-detection PURELY gate-based (`gateN >= gateM`); `phase` plays no role in `kind` (it only feeds the chip LABEL fallback `?? 'done'`). The SAME iteration added an ITERATION NOTE to this very file (lines 22-28) stating done-detection is 'completely independent of `phase`', so the header now self-contradicts, and line 6 perpetuates the exact stale `phase`-in-kind framing FINDING-DA-004 was raised to correct (now reconciled in src/shared/orky-status.ts's heartbeatKind doc-comment, 02-spec.md REQ-009, 03-plan.md TASK-005, and traceability.{json,md} — but not here).
fix: At doc-sync (or the next sanctioned touch), reword line 6 to drop `phase` from the `kind` derivation list, e.g. '`kind` is now DERIVED ... from `needsHuman`/`gateN`/`gateM` (REQ-009 — gate-based done-detection; `phase` feeds only the chip LABEL fallback, never `kind`)', matching the corrected wording already used in heartbeatKind's doc-comment and 02-spec.md REQ-009.

location: src/shared/orky-status.ts:295 (chipLabel `?? 'done'`) + :380 (heartbeatDetail `?? 'done'`) on the stream path / REQ-009 + TEST-041 (tests/shared/orky-heartbeat-status.test.ts:63-67)
claim: This iteration correctly decoupled `kind`'s done-detection from `phase` (now `gateN >= gateM` alone), but left the `?? 'done'` phase-LABEL fallback in chipLabel/heartbeatDetail unchanged on the stream path — and that fallback's SEMANTICS no longer hold there. On 0004's filesystem path a null `phase` is PRODUCED BY `gateFrontier` and means complete, so labelling it 'done' is correct. On the 0014 stream path a null `phase` comes straight off the wire and (post-fix) is decoupled from completeness, so a feature-bearing heartbeat with `phase: null` but INCOMPLETE gates (`gateN < gateM`) derives `kind: 'busy'` yet renders the self-contradictory chip label `'auth-login · done · 5/8'` and detail `'auth-login: done in progress'` (traced directly: heartbeatKind returns 'busy' for 5>=8 false, while chipLabel/heartbeatDetail's `null ?? 'done'` injects the literal 'done'). This is EXACTLY the shape TEST-041 (`BUSY_PHASE_NULL_HEARTBEAT`, added by this same diff) constructs, yet TEST-041 asserts only `kind === 'busy'` / `not 'done'` and never inspects the label or detail — so the contradictory presentation is a case the tests ignore. Real-wire impact is nil (the emitter's `phase = state.phase || d.phase || null` is null only when `gateFrontier` is null, i.e. all gates passed, so phase-null-with-incomplete-gates does not occur on genuine output), hence LOW — but the spec's stated justification for keeping the fallback ('same chipLabel convention 0004 uses for gateFrontier') silently relies on the emitter invariant 'null phase => complete' that Termhalla never enforces or asserts, so a drifted/spoofed heartbeat (cf. DA-001 trust boundary, DA-006 cross-repo drift) would present a status that reads 'done' while being busy. Distinct from QUAL-003 (which flags only the `?? 'done'` code duplication, not its now-mismatched stream-path semantics), QUAL-005/DD-003 (stale doc/comment TEXT, not rendered output).
